sourceMovie.title: Chicago
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Chicago works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Sting
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Chicago clicked for you, The Sting (1973) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Chicago?
faq.answer: Try The Sting, The Blues Brothers, Some Like It Hot—each shares DNA with Chicago while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Chicago?
faq.answer: Chicago (2002) is best described as Comedy, Crime, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Chicago worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
